title: Worktree cleanup only removes the root node_modules link
reasoning: linkWorktreeNodeModules explicitly handles the root node_modules plus nested workspace node_modules paths, but removeWorktree only removes `${worktree}/node_modules` before invoking `git worktree remove --force`. If linkWorktreeNodeModules created nested symlinks such as `packages/foo/node_modules`, those untracked symlinks can remain in the worktree and cause `git worktree remove` to fail or leave directories behind. The catch block then suppresses the failure, so callers can believe cleanup succeeded while the worktree still exists.
recommendation: Call unlinkWorktreeNodeModules(repo, worktree) from removeWorktree, or otherwise remove every path returned by worktreeNodeModulesPaths(repo) before running git worktree remove. Avoid swallowing the final removal failure silently if the worktree still exists.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The integration tests only create and validate a root node_modules symlink, and the remove test removes a worktree that never had nested node_modules symlinks linked into it.
suggestedRegressionTest: Create a fixture repo with a nested workspace node_modules directory, call linkWorktreeNodeModules, then removeWorktree, and assert the worktree path no longer exists.
minimumFixScope: Update removeWorktree to clean the same node_modules symlink set that linkWorktreeNodeModules creates, and add an integration test for nested workspace node_modules cleanup.
